description: Worst-buried backlog ever. The Vegeta node has been carrying a self-documented Caveat for the better part of forty waves explicitly saying 'BT1-036 God Charge Vegeta and TB2-025 Begrudging Respect Vegeta are present in the corpus but unenriched, deferred from appears_on pending enrichment.' I walked the corpus. BT1-036 is vision+trivia+IP-verified, has characters: [vegeta] stamped in frontmatter, is already silently rolled into the node's appears_on list AND the auto-generated Appears-on body section by some janitor pass, but the body prose in three places still says 'three corpus anchors' and BT1-036 is still listed as 'unenriched / deferred.' Classic sync-drift. This is the wave-114 Majin Buu Revived attach precedent applied to the Vegeta node: an unwired anchor — well, a HALF-wired anchor where the structure is correct but the body prose hasn't caught up — for an already-existing node, where the trivia bullets had already documented the canonical character relation. Pulled the strongest single move: stack-attach BT1-036 to the DBS / SS-Blue aspect bullet. The card's trivia is dense Vegeta-thesis material — the Vegeta-route-to-Super-Saiyan-Blue (private training under Whis, no five-Saiyan group ritual) is the canonical individualist-pride articulation, the 'Charge' title encodes the canonical Vegeta-accumulated-ki combat philosophy contrast against Goku's instinctive Kamehameha-release, and the card is Rare in BT1 (12 of 114 set slots) — material I could write the bullet from without inventing. Wrote the SS-Blue aspect bullet, upgraded the aspect header from 'one corpus card' to 'two corpus cards,' corrected the post-aspects-paragraph corpus-anchor-count language, rewrote the Caveats deferred-list note to reflect the new state (BT4-030 At All Costs Vegeta also wants the same body-bulleting work and is now explicitly parked for the next Edgelord pass; TB2-025 stays in the deferred bucket since it's trivia-pending), and corrected the Future Discrete Lair candidate Caveat from a stale 3-anchor / 9-inventory count to the actual 5-enriched / 12-inventory state across the six corpus prints. BT1-036 itself had no ## Connections section so I wrote one fresh pointing at the vegeta character node with the canonical-anchor sentence. No new edge invented; this is the body-prose half of an already-half-existing stack-attach relation. Per swarm-staggering / one-anchor-per-invocation discipline, I did NOT also write BT4-030's aspect bullet — that's a separate stack-attach for the next pass even though the conditions are structurally identical (vision+trivia+IP-verified, frontmatter-wired, body bullet missing).
citations: [Dragon Ball Wiki: Vegeta] — canonical character page covering the Battle of Gods / Resurrection F Super Saiyan Blue acquisition arc
[Kanzenshuu: Vegeta-to-Super-Saiyan-Blue training route under Whis without the five-Saiyan ritual] — already cited in BT1-036's own trivia bullets
[GameRant: Dragon Ball Super Saiyan Blue Explained] — already cited in BT1-036's own trivia bullets for the Toriyama official-name 'Super Saiyan God Super Saiyan' canon

[CBR: Dragon Ball How Vegeta Achieved Super Saiyan God] — already cited in BT1-036's own trivia bullets for the Vegeta-accumulated-ki combat-philosophy contrast against Goku-instinctive-techniques framing
BT1-036's own trivia bullets — four bullets independently established the SS-Blue / individualist-pride / Vegeta-route / accumulated-ki canonical readings well before this Edgelord attach
other_candidates_considered: BT4-030 At All Costs Vegeta stack-attach to the same SS-Blue aspect — vision+trivia+IP-verified, characters: [vegeta] stamped, frontmatter and auto-section already wired, body bullet still missing. Structurally identical to the BT1-036 case. Deferred to the next Edgelord pass per one-anchor-per-invocation discipline. Will likely ship next wave; should trigger the SS-Blue aspect header upgrade from 'two corpus cards' to 'three corpus cards' and another Discrete Lair candidate count refresh.

TB2-025 Begrudging Respect Vegeta full body-attach — blocked. Card is vision-passed only; trivia + IP-verification still outstanding. Frontmatter has characters: [vegeta] stamped (correct) but the body-bullet write-up is deferred until trivia-pass lands per the trunks / son-goku enriched-anchors-only precedent. Janitor wishlist candidate when trivia batch hits TB2.

BT2 Union Force unwired list per the wave-114 and wave-115 carry-forwards (bt2-012 Lightning Speed Vegito, bt2-013 Repeated Force Vegito, bt2-018 Videl, bt2-052 Yajirobe, bt2-061 Gowasu, bt2-115 Salza) — none are unwired anchors on already-existing nodes in the same one-pass-fix sense BT1-036 is. Each would require either a Mr. Nodeley fresh-node pass or node-density-threshold satisfaction. Vegito specifically (BT2-012 + BT2-013) is the strongest Mr. Nodeley candidate — Vegito is canonically the Goku+Vegeta Potara fusion from the Buu Saga and DBS Future Trunks Saga, two corpus prints in the same set, no node yet. Flagged for the next Nodeley pass.
Cinderella cycle Discrete Lair pre-assembly cohesion-edge — eld-cinderella-cycle.md exists as the anchor; the cohesion edges are already drawn. No outstanding Edgelord work on that cluster.

Liliana Vess / War Triumph cycle from wave 110-112 — already comprehensive at the 2-anchor scope; canonical extension card (Liliana's Triumph WAR-98) is not in corpus. No wireable edge until CSV ingest.

Krillin <-> Android 18 character-to-character relation, Jace <-> Liliana, Chandra <-> Bolas, Gideon <-> Liliana — still parked behind the 'defer character-to-character edge pending framework formalization' rule from the wave-71 krillin commission note. Same disposition as wave 115.

Single Strike mechanic character/cycle character-layer node — flagged in wave 115 as a Mr. Nodeley candidate; the symbol-layer single-strike-emblem.md already does the structural work via appears_on. Not Edgelord's call until the layer-sibling question is decided.
